_about: Example config for universal_civ5_pipeline.py — the ART / DDS stage of a Civ V
Brave New World custom-civ build. This script builds the icon atlases, alpha/team
icons, unit flags, Dawn of Man image, map image, and leader scene, then writes the
IconTextureAtlas XML, a DDS manifest, a QA report, and per-asset previews.
It does NOT generate Civilization.sql / Leader.sql / Trait.sql / Text.sql / Colors.sql
/ the .modinfo, etc. Those gameplay tables belong to the separate SQL/text/.modinfo
stage of the builder. The full intended civ is documented (for that stage) under the
_civ_design_reference key below, which this art pipeline ignores.
JSON has no comment syntax, so all explanatory text lives in _-prefixed keys that the
pipeline does not read.
Paths are relative to the directory you run the script from. Put your source PNGs at the
listed Input/ paths (filenames must NOT carry trailing numeric suffixes like _1 or _256).
Run:
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config LITHUANIA_CUSTOM.dds.json --check-only
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config LITHUANIA_CUSTOM.dds.json
Atlas layout produced by this build (fixed in code, not set here):
Normal icon atlas: 4x4 grid (16 cells); sizes 256,128,80,64,45,32 (256 atlas = 1024x1024).
Unit flag atlas:   8x8 grid, 32px cells (256x256).
Leader scene:      1024x768 (civ5_leader_scene_4x3) or 1280x720 (civ5_leader_scene_16x9).
Dawn of Man:       1024x768.
The IconTextureAtlas XML it emits already matches this 4x4 / 16-cell layout.
_source_art_naming: Civilization alpha  -> alpha_icon   (white-on-black glyph; kept strictly at 0.78)
Unique unit icon    -> units[].source        (standard icon, occupancy 0.82)
Unique unit flag    -> flags[].source         (white silhouette, 24x24 box in 32px cell = 0.75)
Unique building icon -> buildings[].source    (standard icon, occupancy 0.82)
_icon_fit_policy_note: auto = cover full-bleed/disc/photo art to fill the circle, contain glyph art at vanilla occupancy. Use 'contain' to force strict occupancy for everything, 'cover' to always fill.

_note: NOT read by the art pipeline. This documents the full civ requested in the original
spec so the SQL/text/.modinfo stage of the builder can consume it. All names are
placeholders and all replacement targets / type names MUST be validated against the
vanilla BNW reference database before generating SQL. Any trait/unit/building effect
that cannot be mapped to a verified table/column belongs in UNIMPLEMENTED_EFFECTS.md.

mod.name: Kingdom of Lithuania (Custom)
mod.teaser: Adds the Kingdom of Lithuania under Vytautas.
mod.description: A custom Brave New World civilization: the Kingdom of Lithuania, led by Vytautas, with the Grand Duchy trait, the Bajoras unique unit, and the Hillfort unique building.

civilization.starting_bias_note: Set CivilizationType start bias only if supported by the verified DB schema.

leader._flavor_note: Use only FlavorTypes that exist in the vanilla DB. Values are placeholders 1-10.

trait.description_text: PLACEHOLDER trait effect — describe in plain language, then map to verified Trait columns or mark Lua-required.

candidate_effects.mapping_status: VERIFY — confirm against the Traits table columns before implementing; otherwise UNIMPLEMENTED_EFFECTS.md.

unique_units._replacement_note: Confirm the exact UnitType and the UnitClass it belongs to in the vanilla DB; use Civilization_UnitClassOverrides as verified by reference examples.

overrides_placeholder.MoveRate_note: Leave 0 / omit to inherit the replaced unit's defaults; only override verified columns.

unique_buildings._replacement_note: Confirm the BuildingType and its BuildingClass in the vanilla DB; use Civilization_BuildingClassOverrides as verified by reference examples.

_civ_design_reference._city_names_note: 16 placeholder names. Not asserted to be historically accurate; edit before use.

colors._format_note: Civ V Colors are stored as 0.0-1.0 RGBA floats in the Colors table. Values below are placeholders.
